g.text: EFFR and SOFR spreads show whether market rates are pressing against the administered-rate floor.
1. Pricing symptom: overnight rates versus IORB
Reserves move against Treasury cash, ON RRP usage, and currency demand on the Fed liability side.
2. Reserve mechanics: liability-side drains and buffers
Repo assets and ON RRP usage show the facility footprint behind funding-market implementation.
Total assets, Treasury holdings, and reserves provide the broad regime context.
Read the board clockwise from the pricing symptom to the plumbing mechanism: overnight-rate spreads to IORB show stress or looseness,
reserve-side liabilities explain mechanical drains and buffers, repo and ON RRP show the operational facility footprint, and the final panel
gives the long-run balance-sheet regime backdrop.
